explanation: Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories.

discriminators: RECORDING-TECHNIQUE LABEL, not an acoustic class. All five FOLEY subcategories carry one identical explanation — "Used for 'performed' official and very clean Foley done to picture. For your own wild recordings use FOOTSTEPS and OBJECT categories." — so FOLEY is defined by where and why a sound was recorded, never by what it sounds like.

Content is cloth rustle and movement — acoustically the same object as CLOTH-MOVEMENT, CLOTH-FLAP and CLOTH-HANDLE, where a wild recording of the identical performance would be filed. The morphology field describes that content; it says nothing about the FOLEY label.

One real but weak cue exists, and it identifies a Foley STAGE, not a subcategory: stage recordings have an unusually low noise floor, a dead room (very short decay_time_seconds_60db, minimal reverb tail) and near-mono / narrow imaging (low stereo_width). When those three co-occur the file was probably shot on a Foley stage — but they say nothing about whether it is CLOTH, FEET, HANDS, MISC or PROP. No priors are declared: the tier is provenance_only, so the signal weight is zero by construction, and encoding a stage cue as a subcategory prior would misrepresent a recording condition as a content claim.
Routing among the five is carried entirely by synonyms plus the FOOTSTEPS / OBJECTS cross-reference in the explanation. Signal analysis must never assert this subcategory.

Content is footsteps — indistinguishable from FOOTSTEPS-HUMAN, which the explanation itself points you to for wild recordings. A Foley walker on a stage and a person walking on location produce the same impulse train; only the room and the intent differ.

Content is hand contact — pats, slaps, grabs, rubs, claps. Acoustically HUMAN-SKIN, OBJECTS handling and CLOTH-HANDLE. No feature separates a performed hand pat from a real one.

Content is arbitrary — a catch-all inside a provenance bucket, and therefore doubly unassertable. Its only synonym is 'Miscellaneous'. It is reached by abstention within FOLEY, and FOLEY itself is reached only by text.

Content is object handling and impacts — acoustically the OBJECTS category (and WOOD / METAL / GLASS handling), which the explanation points you to for wild recordings. A performed prop set-down is a set-down.
